site.title: Chen Hetian — Young Artist
site.description: Chen Hetian, born in 1990 in Xiamen, Fujian, is a young artist known for his intense colors and saturated compositions. His notable works include the Soul, Eye, Between Wandering, and Implication series.
hero.name: Chen Hetian
hero.subtitle: Intense colors, saturated compositions — using stories as brushes and faith as pigments, freely moving between reality and spirit
about.name: Chen Hetian
about.subtitle: Young Artist · Oil Painting
about.quote: For painting, I love it from the bottom of my heart. I never paint for no reason — there must be a story to tell, to move others.
about.bio.0: Born in October 1990 in Xiamen, Fujian (Xiang'an District), currently works and lives in Xiamen. Graduated from Guizhou Normal University in 2015 with a bachelor's degree in oil painting.
about.bio.1: During his university years, he was deeply influenced by the vibrant colors and mysterious totems of ethnic minorities such as the Miao and Buyi peoples, which directly inspired his graduation work "Soul" series. After graduation, he experienced a period of career confusion, working as a substitute teacher and running painting training classes, but he always persisted in his artistic creation.
about.bio.2: In 2016, his work was sold for the first time in Shanghai, and subsequently he participated in exhibitions both domestically and internationally. In 2022, Huawei purchased more than 40 of his works at once, with a total value exceeding 1 million yuan, becoming a key turning point in his artistic career, after which he became a full-time painter.
about.bio.3: In February 2026, he co-founded an exhibition space with friends in Xiamen's Shapowei, creating a platform for exchange between local and outside young artists.
about.philosophy.0.title: Painting Style
about.philosophy.0.content: Primarily working in oil painting, with intense colors and saturated compositions, his works are full of tension and explosive power. He excels at using thick impasto technique, adding sifted fine sand to the paint, creating a three-dimensional texture similar to wool fabric and a sculptural effect through layering.
about.philosophy.1.title: Cultural Roots
about.philosophy.1.content: His creations often incorporate totems and symbolic elements drawn from the culture of Guizhou's ethnic minorities (Buyei, Miao). The style of his mixed media works is refined and generalized, with all totem symbols in the paintings crowding, repelling, or echoing each other, containing metaphors and presenting a naive and pure enthusiasm.
about.philosophy.2.title: Spiritual Sustenance
about.philosophy.2.content: He views art as a spiritual 'supplement' and sustenance, a way to compensate for the lacking reality of life. He considers artistic creation a kind of 'faith' and 'spiritual utopia,' while emphasizing that one must still face reality.
about.background.0.title: Education
about.background.0.items.0: 2011–2015 Guizhou Normal University · Oil Painting · Bachelor's Degree
about.background.1.title: Career Experience
about.background.1.items.0: 2015 Graduated from university, experienced career confusion
about.background.1.items.1: 2015–2016 Served as a substitute teacher at school
about.background.1.items.3: 2016 Shanghai exhibition, works sold for the first time
about.background.1.items.4: 2022 Huawei collected over 40 works, became a full-time painter
about.background.1.items.5: 2026 Co-founder of Xiamen Shapowei Exhibition Space
about.background.2.title: Representative Series
about.recognition.quote: The "Soul" series is the essence of spiritual transcendence, a record of temporal culture.
about.recognition.items.0.title: Huawei Collection
about.recognition.items.0.content: In 2022, Huawei Company purchased over 40 works by Chen Hetian at once, with a total value exceeding 1 million yuan. This was a key turning point in his artistic career, after which he became a full-time painter, dedicating himself fully to creation.
about.recognition.items.1.title: Exhibition Footprints
about.recognition.items.1.content: His works have been exhibited in major cities across China including Beijing, Shanghai, Shenzhen, Wuhan, and Nanjing, as well as in art institutions and galleries in New York, USA, Morocco, and other locations.
about.awards.0.items.0: Radiant Years 7th National Art Education Achievement Exhibition · Student Group First Prize
about.awards.1.items.0: 5th Guizhou Province Graduate Art Works Exhibition · Silver Award
about.awards.1.items.1: 7th "Chengfeng Cup" Guizhou Province College Student Art Works Competition · Third Prize
about.awards.2.items.0: First Chinese and Foreign Art Literature Award · Nomination Award (Yun Series)
about.studio.description: My studio is located in Shapowei, Xiamen, an old fishing port nestled by the sea. Morning sunlight streams through the floor-to-ceiling windows onto the canvas, while the salty sea breeze mingles with the scent of paint, creating the palette of my work. This is my intimate space for dialogue with color—each piece begins in the stillness here and gradually takes shape on the canvas. The studio is filled with various oil paints, palettes, and unfinished canvases, while the walls are adorned with art books and exhibition posters collected over the years, documenting my creative journey from Guizhou to Xiamen.

contact.collaboration.quote: As long as I can make a living, I will never give up painting.
contact.collaboration.items.0.title: Artwork Collection
contact.collaboration.items.0.content: If you are interested in collecting Chen Hetian's works, please feel free to contact us via WeChat. We will provide artwork catalogs, pricing, and collection process guidance.
contact.collaboration.items.1.title: Exhibition Invitation
contact.collaboration.items.1.content: Curators from museums, galleries, and art spaces both domestically and internationally are welcome to contact us for exhibition cooperation. We provide complete artwork proposals and display recommendations.
contact.collaboration.items.2.title: Media Interview
contact.collaboration.items.2.content: We accept interviews and filming cooperation from art media, cultural magazines, and documentary teams, sharing creative philosophies and artistic stories.

categories.worksSeries.5: Dare to Ask Where the Road Leads
works.3.title: Eye · No.1
works.3.description: The 'Eye' series, with gazing as its theme, explores the relationship between seeing and being seen, with powerful tension and explosive force in the composition.
works.4.title: Between Wandering No. 1
works.4.description: The 'Between Wandering' series explores the boundary between existence and nothingness, with colors flowing and colliding in the composition, creating unique visual tension.
works.5.title: Connotation No. 1
works.5.description: The 'Connotation' series regards art as a spiritual sustenance, using intense colors to compensate for the spiritually deficient real life.
works.6.title: Enlightenment 1
works.6.series: Dare to Ask Where the Road Leads
works.6.description: The 'Dare to Ask Where the Road Leads' series on paper, contemplating life's direction and the choice of artistic path.
works.7.title: Soul No. 3
works.7.material: Oil on canvas, pigment with fine sand
works.7.description: Continuing the exploration of the 'Soul' series, with intense colors and full composition, totem symbols crowding, repelling or echoing each other, containing metaphors.
works.8.title: Multitude
works.8.description: Exploring the relationship between individuals and groups, thick impasto technique creates a three-dimensional texture, giving the painting a mural-like heaviness.
works.9.title: Impression of Dahongpao
works.9.description: Inspired by Wuyi Mountain Dahongpao, paper-based sketch presents the fusion of tea culture and landscape imagery.
works.10.title: Busy Society No. 1
works.10.description: The 'Busy Society' series presents the busyness and anxiety of contemporary society through mixed media installations, reflecting on the pace of modern life.
